message:
deps: bump toml 0.8 -> 1.1.2 (spec-1.1.0)

Major bump (first 1.x release). The toml-rs 0.9 → 1.0 rewrite introduced:

- Serde and std are now opt-in default features (still on by default here)
- `Deserializer::new`/`ValueDeserializer::new` deprecated in favor of
  `Deserializer::parse`
- Serializer accepts `&mut Buffer` instead of `&mut String`
- Order preservation now requires the `preserve_order` feature
- `toml_edit` crate was absorbed, `toml_write` renamed to `toml_writer`
- New `toml_parser` helper crate added

The only consumer in this workspace
(crates/ffs-harness/src/benchmark_taxonomy.rs) uses the stable surface:
`toml::from_str(&text)` and `toml::de::Error`. Both still exist and
behave identically in 1.1 — the taxonomy loader does not depend on key
ordering, so the ordering-preservation default change is a non-issue.

Lockfile cleanup: toml_edit/toml_write replaced by toml_parser/toml_writer;
serde_spanned, toml_datetime, winnow bumped in lockstep.

Verification:
  - rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets: clean
  - rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-harness --lib: 312 passed / 0 failed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
